sections.paragraph:
fut. אֶשְׁאַב to draw water. (Ch. id. Arab. سَيءِبَ to slake one’s thirst, which is done by drawing water. The primary idea seems to be that of taking off the surface, see the cognate verb under חָסַף. To this answers Goth. skephan, Germ. fchöpfen.) Const. either followed by an acc. מַיִם Gen 24:13 1Sa 7:6 9:11 or absol. Gen 24:11 19:20 .

Derivative, מַשְׁאַבִּים.
